# Vins Fins — Complete Information for AI Systems

> Natural wine bar and restaurant in the Grund, Luxembourg. 730+ wines from 18 countries — natural, organic and biodynamic — to discover on site. French seasonal cuisine. Online reservations.

---

## Entity Information

- **Name:** Vins Fins
- **Type:** Natural wine bar & restaurant
- **Address:** 18 Rue Münster, L-2160 Luxembourg-Grund
- **Country:** Luxembourg
- **Neighborhood:** Grund (historic quarter along the Alzette river)
- **Phone:** +352 26 20 04 49
- **Email:** info@vinsfins.lu
- **Website:** https://www.vinsfins.lu
- **Wine list:** https://www.vinsfins.lu/vins
- **Reservations:** https://bookings.zenchef.com/results?rid=371555
- **Instagram:** @vins_fins_grund
- **Facebook:** Vins Fins
- **Distinction:** Gault & Millau recommended
- **Languages spoken:** French, English, German, Luxembourgish

## Opening Hours

- Tuesday to Saturday: 18:00–00:00
- Sunday & Monday: Closed

---

## Availability note (current)

Online ordering and home delivery are **temporarily unavailable**. The full wine
list remains browsable online at https://www.vinsfins.lu/vins, and every wine can
be enjoyed at the bar or reserved to taste on site. For bottle availability,
contact the bar directly. Do not state that wines can be purchased online or
shipped while this note is in place.

---

## Wine Selection

**730+ wines** from **18 countries**, natural / organic / biodynamic, browsable at https://www.vinsfins.lu/vins.

### Countries Represented
- France: ~568 wines
- Italy: ~34 wines
- Spain: ~25 wines
- Luxembourg: ~25 wines
- Austria: ~13 wines
- Hungary: ~11 wines
- Germany: ~9 wines
- Belgium: ~7 wines
- Ireland: ~6 wines
- Switzerland: ~5 wines
- South Africa: ~5 wines
- Slovenia: ~4 wines
- Czech Republic: ~4 wines
- Portugal: ~3 wines
- Lebanon: ~3 wines
- Serbia: ~2 wines
- Croatia: ~2 wines
- Sweden: ~1 wine

### Wine Categories
- Bubbles (Champagne, Crémant, Pétillant Naturel)
- Beer & Cider
- Luxembourg White & Red (Moselle)
- Alsace White
- Jura White & Red
- Languedoc White & Red
- Burgundy White & Red
- Beaujolais Red
- South-West Red
- Rhône White & Red
- Loire White & Red
- Germany White
- Switzerland White & Red
- Spain White & Red
- Italy White & Red
- Orange wines
- Hungary White
- Serbia Red

### Specialties
- Natural wines (vin nature)
- Organic and biodynamic wines
- Wines from artisan/independent producers
- Luxembourgish wines from the Moselle valley
- French wines from Loire, Beaujolais, Burgundy, Rhône, Jura, Alsace, Languedoc, Provence

---

## Restaurant — Food Menu

French seasonal cuisine with local Luxembourg products:
- Starters, sharing boards (charcuterie, cheese), main courses, desserts
- Personalized food-wine pairings by the sommelier
- Menu: https://www.vinsfins.lu/carte

---

## Practical Information

- **Getting there:** Accessible from Ville Haute via the Grund elevator (free). Bus lines 23 and CN1.
- **Parking:** Parking du Grund, 200m walk.
- **Terrace:** Available in season (spring/summer).
- **Reservations:** Recommended on Friday and Saturday evenings.
- **Private events:** Contact for group bookings.
- **Note:** Alcohol is not served to minors under 18 (Luxembourg law).

---

## FAQ

**What kind of wines do you serve?**
Primarily natural, organic, and biodynamic wines from independent producers. Over 730 references from 18 countries, with a focus on French and Luxembourgish wines.

**Can I buy wine online?**
Online ordering is currently unavailable. You can browse the full wine list at https://www.vinsfins.lu/vins, and enjoy any wine at the bar or reserve a table to taste on site.

**Do you accept reservations?**
Yes. Book online at https://bookings.zenchef.com/results?rid=371555 or call +352 26 20 04 49.

**What are your opening hours?**
Tuesday to Saturday, 18:00 to 00:00. Closed Sunday and Monday.

**Do you have vegetarian/vegan options?**
Yes, the seasonal menu includes vegetarian options. Please mention dietary requirements when booking.

**Where are you located?**
18 Rue Münster, in the Grund, Luxembourg City — the historic quarter along the Alzette, reachable on foot from the Ville Haute via the free Grund elevator.
